video.title: Sprinters Battle On Flattest Stage | Giro D'Italia 2022 Stage 11 Highlights

transcript.clean_text: beautiful emilia romagna was on show on stage 11 of the giro d'italia on stage 10 it had been history made by binyam gomai of eritrea the first man from his nation to win a stage of a grand tour the first black african to do so but unfortunately it would also be the last act of the race for him young gil mai a global superstar after winning him to evoke him and this stage of the jiro was hit by a prosecco cork on the podium and would be unable to take part further in the race a couple of hours in hospital and some treatment he was advised to go home and rest so no girl but 165 riders including vincenzo nibali kelebion and the rest were all ready to start a pan flat stage that should have been one for the sprinters but the wind was blowing and there was a little bit of uncertainty in the air a strong cross tailwind forecast for most of the day all the way up the emiliano romanolo plane a race up to bologna then a deviation instead of the usual trip through modena into back roads and finishing in bridge emilia for the first time in five seasons fernando gabiria being the winner of the stage in 2017 who would succeed in and would it end up being a big bunch sprint but the fast explosive start as anticipated didn't materialize two riders got up the road by the time it came to the intermediate sprint philly portalani beating lucarastelli who was on gero debut behind arnold de mar mott kamadish giacomo nizolo going for the intermediate points kavadish winning that little bunch kick and taliani adding to his lead in the intermediate sprint competition just under 100 kilometers to go and the gap was coming down facing through bologna the nerves were all over the peloton and just out of bologna the breakaway was caught already after having had a gap of five minutes just under 90 k's to go in the first splits appeared caleb young was caught out early on simon yates as well hugh carthy had to work to get back on but it was all back together with around 80 k's to go again then quick step would try in your screened ears would never be far away alpacine fenix gave it a go but there's nothing doing in the early efforts second intermediate sprint with just over 75 kilometers to go this time for the bonus seconds richard carapaz winning it taking three seconds and moving up into second place on the gc 12 behind the leader juanpe lopez dresdebont would then go off the front more than 50 kilometers remain when he had a small gap and that gap would grow to a minute and a half he was still out there inside the final 10 kilometers the sprinters not wanting to launch the lead out too early 20 seconds with five kilometers was he starting to dream perhaps but when the lead outs were launched just over one kilometer remained when the former belgian champion was caught and it would be as expected a bunch sprint obama fdg and quick step alpha vinyl looking for that final corner maxi would be there for uae he'd look around look for his sprinter but fernando gavirio was following other wheels and they proved to be good wheels he'd be right at the front thick of the action looking like he was about to win consonant to his right demarto is left but look at alberto dainese wasn't even meant to be for him on the day kiss ball not feeling good dainese given a free card he was led out by human baldi and dainese coming off the wheel of the sprinters to win for the first time in over two long years a first ever world tour win a first grand tour stage win only the third win in the 24 year old sprinter's career the first italian victory on day 11 of this giro d'italia alberto dainese making a name for himself and winning for dsm he beat fernando gaviria simone consonant demar ewan and cavendish were all beaten as well returns modelo bauhaus and narsen completing the top 10. juan pelopez held on to the pink jersey despite nervous moments in the wind he now has a 12-second gap over not only zhuawa maida but richard carapaz ii with those bonus seconds he moved up in to second balder and hindley complete the top five as we go climbing again heading from parma to genoa there'll be a somber moment as we return to the paso del bocco for the first time since the tragic death of walter whalen 11 years ago and a few hills towards the end a return to genoa for the first time in seven years and lia viviani the last winner in the lantern city an interesting day an unpredictable day perhaps one for the breakaway we'll have to wait to find out the longest stage of the judo at 204 kilometers you'll be able to catch it as always on gcn plus eurosport and discovery plus it'll be live and on demand on all of our digital platforms
